What Goes Wrong: Re-fitting a new random matrix at query time gives a different subspace than the one used at ingest. Query and DB vectors become incomparable — recall silently drops to near zero.
Fix: Serialize the fitted projector (pickle) immediately after fit_transform. Load the same object at query time. Never re-fit on new or dummy data.

Pitfall: 2. Choosing k below the JL bound
What Goes Wrong: JL guarantees hold only when k >= 4 ln(n) / (eps^2/2 - eps^3/3). Below this threshold, distance distortion is unbounded — you lose the mathematical guarantee entirely, not just slightly.
Fix: Call jl_min_dimension(n, eps) before choosing k. If storage forces a smaller k, accept a larger eps and report it honestly.

What Goes Wrong: JL preserves Euclidean distances, not cosine similarities. If your vector DB uses cosine distance on un-normalised vectors, the JL guarantee does not apply.
Fix: L2-normalise all vectors before projecting. After normalisation, cosine similarity equals dot product, and Euclidean distances are monotonically related — the JL bound then applies.

What Goes Wrong: The required k scales as O(log n). A k that was JL-safe at n=5,000 may not be safe at n=500,000. Stale projectors also cannot project new documents into a consistent subspace without re-ingesting everything.
Fix: Re-evaluate jl_min_dimension(n, eps) whenever n grows by an order of magnitude. Plan for periodic re-indexing if the DB grows significantly.

What Goes Wrong: JL assumes worst-case (adversarial) point sets. Real embeddings often lie on a low-dimensional manifold. Recall may be fine at k << JL bound or may collapse unpredictably — the guarantee does not apply in either direction for structured data.
Fix: Always validate recall empirically across a held-out query set. Do not rely on the theoretical bound alone for production deployments.

Pitfall: 6. Projecting query with a different k than the DB
What Goes Wrong: If the DB was indexed at k=512 but the query is projected to k=256 (or vice versa), the vectors have different lengths and the distance computation is undefined or silently wrong.
Fix: Store k alongside the projector. At query time, assert that the query dimension matches the collection dimension before searching.
